content:
Hi,
Recently I've been working on the SAR plugin (code & gui) and I changed a lot of texts which are not longer translated.

I was wondering if :

  • there was a procedure/guide that explains how to update the main .pot and all .po translations files with the new texts ?
  • Is it done via poedit or externals scripts ?
  • How to push it to crowdin ? How to retrieve the translated .po files back to the repo ?
  • How to "compile" all of the translations ?

You need to build the sar-pot-update target (Eg. using make sar-pot-update on Unix systems. You will find out that po/SAR_PI.potfile is named wrong and needs to be renamed all lowercase for the stuff to actually work on case sensitive filesystems).
Then that file needs to be uploaded to Crowdin (If you don't have privileges for that, let me or @Håkan Svensson know and we will do it for you)
Once you want the new .po files with translations, just download them from Crowdin (Build & Download button at https://crowdin.com/project/opencpn/translations - Again ask me or Hakan if you need help with that), copy yours to the po directory and commit them to the plugin sources.

@Kupofty Or if you like pls tell me where the source is and I can fix a .pot file to Crowdin. Then we make a note on CF so that translators know about it and can contribute according to their desire and ability. After a week or two I can let Crowdin build, download and PR the result to your source?

The only script in the po folder of SAR plugin has some commented parts and is only updating the POTFILEs.in : 

#bin/bash
cd ..
#//ls src/*.cpp src/*.h > po/POTFILES.in
#//cd po
#//xgettext -a -C ../src/route_pi.cpp -o route_pi.pot

#cd plugins/{$plugin}/
find src/ -name \*.cpp -or -name \*.h -or -name \*.hpp >po/POTFILES.in

I was thinking of using the one from meteovache_pi :

#!/bin/sh
cd ..;xgettext --add-comments=TRANSLATORS --force-po --from-code=iso-8859-1 --output=po/xxxxx_pi.pot --keyword=_ --width=80 --files-from=po/POTFILES.in

Is this one correct or is there a better/approved model for this script ?

I have an Crowdin account and already contributed to OCPN translation but I doubt I have any privilege.
https://crowdin.com/project/opencpn/translations : Don't have access to this either.

First you have to add all source files containing string(s) to translate to POTFILES.in
Then run the project create the .pot file.
Then upload that to Crowdin.
When we think we got as much translations as possible for the moment we download the .po files and publish the plugin.
Also described here.
Again if I get the source you're working on I can be of help with the above if you like.

The instructions in the opencpn-manuals are quite clear, thanks.

Will send you a message once the sar_pi.pot is ready.
Just want to check one last time that everything is correct and confirm with Mike.
